task: Self-contained task: do not consult /Users/feijun/.codex/memories or emit memory citations; all required review input is in this prompt and local files. Supersede failed v8 and fix every finding from review-core-policy-state-v5, v6, and v7. Scope only scripts/legion_core.py and tests/test_legion_core.py. Required fixes: (1) worker results must be strict whole-file JSON, not prose-wrapped JSON; validate schema-equivalent required keys status, summary, files_touched, verification, findings, risks and key types before accepting any terminal status; reject missing keys and wrong types with clear failed status. (2) status/read-only paths must not init_state or create state dirs/files on fresh missing state. (3) wait_readiness must fail when rejected_expected is non-empty and must preserve/report the original explicit expected list; readiness orders must keep ordered L2s visible as missing/rejected rather than Ready 0/0. (4) readiness must not count stale direct L2 commanders whose tmux session is missing/inaccessible; add regression coverage. (5) DELIVERY_ROLES must include every write-capable role including rescue, or derive delivery from workspace-write policy, so direct L1 no-delivery and scope checks apply. (6) delivery roles/branches must require non-empty scope. (7) normalize scopes relative to project root, reject path traversal/out-of-project scopes, and detect exact/ancestor/descendant overlaps such as src vs src/app.py and ./src/app.py vs src/app.py. (8) make campaign scope ownership transactional across active-scope read, conflict check, branch commander reuse, and task insertion; avoid two concurrent campaigns claiming same scope. (9) branch commander reuse must require provider compatibility or refuse/refresh wrong-provider branch commanders. (10) repair_dependents must reject missing or failed/blocked replacement_task_id without mutating registry/events. (11) repair must recursively re-evaluate/unblock transitive blocked dependents whose dependencies become satisfiable after replacement. (12) preserve existing v5 behavior: L1 corps routing, provider defaults, structured completion gate, inbox-before-tmux, registry/inbox/event locks, explicit reconcile, message ids, monotonic terminal states. Add regression tests for every item. Run targeted unit tests and the full unittest suite if the environment allows; if full suite is blocked by tempdir permissions, report exact failure and run all no-temp targeted tests you can. Final output must be only one JSON object.
